perturbation.original_text: Borrower shall use the Information solely in its credit, risk management and net capital processes, including, without limitation, functions such as concentration limit monitor ing, compliance or legal review, or senior management oversight as Borrower may reasonably deem necessary to comply wit h regulatory requirements including, without limitation, regulatory capital computation, regulatory reporting and concentration limit monitoring .
perturbation.changed_text: Borrower shall use the Information solely in its credit, risk management and net capital processes, including, without limitation, functions such as concentration limit monitoring. Borrower is not responsible for compliance or legal review of the information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the borrower's responsibility for compliance or legal review could lead to violations of regulatory reporting requirements.
perturbation.law_explanation: This section requires broker-dealers to preserve records of compliance and legal reviews, which would be undermined if the borrower is explicitly not responsible for such reviews.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.17a-4 - Records to be preserved by certain exchange members, brokers and dealers. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.17a-4 - Records to be preserved by certain exchange members, brokers and dealers. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 240.17a-4 Records to be preserved by certain exchange members, brokers and dealers. This section applies to the following types of entities: A member of a national securities exchange who transacts a business in securities directly with others than members of a national securities exchange ; a broker or dealer who transacts a business in securities through the medium of a member of a national securities exchange ; a broker or dealer, including an OTC derivatives dealer as that term is defined in § 240.3b-12 , registered pursuant to section 15 of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78 o ); a security-based swap dealer registered pursuant to section 15F of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78 o -10) that is also a broker or dealer, including an OTC derivatives dealer , registered pursuant to section 15 of the Act; and a major security-based swap participant registered pursuant to section 15F of the Act that is also a broker or dealer, including an OTC derivatives dealer , registered pursuant to section 15 of the Act. Section 240.18a-6 (rather than this section) applies to the following types of entities: A security-based swap dealer registered pursuant to section 15F of the Act that is not also a broker or dealer, including an OTC derivatives dealer , registered pursuant to section 15 of the Act; and a major security-based swap participant registered pursuant to section 15F of the Act that is not also a broker or dealer, including an OTC derivatives dealer , registered pursuant to section 15 of the Act. (a) Every member , broker or dealer subject to § 240.17a-3 must preserve for a period of not less than 6 years, the first two years
perturbation.justification: The modified text directly contradicts the implied legal obligation for the borrower to be responsible for compliance and legal review of the information, which could undermine the record-keeping requirements as per 17 CFR § 240.17a-4. By stating that the borrower is 'not responsible for compliance or legal review of the information,' the changed text creates a situation where there is no clear responsibility for ensuring regulatory compliance. This contradicts the broader regulatory expectation that broker-dealers must preserve records related to compliance and legal reviews.

perturbation.original_text: Borrower shall maintain the Information in confidence and shall not disclose the information to any third party with out the consent of Lending Agent; provided, however, that Borrower may disclose the Information to the extent required by Sample 2 subpoena, court order, request from a regulatory body with jurisdiction over Borrower, or as otherwise required by law or regulation.
perturbation.changed_text: Borrower shall maintain the Information in confidence and shall not disclose the information to any third party with out the consent of Lending Agent; Borrower cannot disclose the information, even if required by subpoena, court order, request from a regulatory body with jurisdiction over Borrower, or as otherwise required by law or regulation.
perturbation.explanation: This modification prohibits disclosure even when legally mandated, potentially leading to legal penalties for non-compliance.
perturbation.law_explanation: This section dictates requirements for mandatory disclosure in securities litigation, making it illegal to contractually prevent such disclosure.

perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original contract and introduces a legal contradiction. The original text allowed disclosure of information when required by law (e.g., subpoena, court order). The changed text explicitly prohibits disclosure even when legally mandated, creating a situation where the Borrower would be in breach of contract for complying with a legal order. This directly opposes the implied expectation that contracts should not require illegal actions.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its conflict of law principles.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to its conflict of law principles.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law to Delaware may conflict with regulations that apply specifically to financial institutions operating in New York and dealing with New York-based clients.
perturbation.law_explanation: Regulations specific to New York financial services (NYCRR 300.2), governing cybersecurity and data protection standards would not automatically apply if the governing law is changed to Delaware, potentially creating non-compliance.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Message - Unofficial New York Codes, Rules and Regulations This document is not available on Westlaw.
perturbation.justification: The change in governing law from New York to Delaware introduces a legal contradiction because it may allow the contract to avoid compliance with New York-specific regulations. The Law Explanation specifically mentions NYCRR 300.2, which governs cybersecurity and data protection standards for New York financial services. Scraped Law Snippet 1 lists 'Title 3 - BANKING' and 'Title 23 - FINANCIAL SERVICES' under New York Codes, Rules, and Regulations, indicating that New York has specific regulations for these sectors. By changing the governing law to Delaware, these New York-specific regulations might not automatically apply, leading to potential non-compliance, which directly falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations hereunder shall expire upon the earlier to occur of (a) ___ year s from the termination date of the last loan of securities outstanding under the Borrowing Agreement or (b) the date upon which the Information ceases to be confidential as set forth above.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations hereunder shall expire immediately upon the termination date of the last loan of securities outstanding under the Borrowing Agreement or (b) the date upon which the Information ceases to be confidential as set forth above.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the grace period after the termination date of the last loan to allow the borrower some time to still fulfill their obligations violates the right to remedy.
perturbation.law_explanation: This contradicts the principle of 'right to cure' under the UCC, which allows a seller (here, the lending agent providing information) a reasonable time to correct any deficiencies or non-conformities. Eliminating any grace period prevents such correction, violating commercial law principles.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-508. Cure by Seller of Improper Tender or Delivery; Replacement. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-508. Cure by Seller of Improper Tender or Delivery; Replacement. (1) Where any tender or delivery by the seller is rejected because non-conforming and the time for performance has not yet expired, the seller may seasonably notify the buyer of his intention to cure and may then within the contract time make a conforming delivery. (2) Where the buyer rejects a non-conforming tender which the seller had reasonable grounds to believe would be acceptable with or without money allowance the seller may if he seasonably notifies the buyer have a further reasonable time to substitute a conforming tender. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modification removes a grace period for fulfilling obligations, conflicting with the principle of 'right to cure.' Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing UCC § 2-508, allows a seller time to correct deficiencies. The changed text eliminates this 'further reasonable time,' creating a direct contradiction by preventing the lending agent from rectifying any non-conformities, thus violating the right to cure.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding between the parties and supersedes any and all prior agreements and understandings, oral or written, relating to th e subject matter hereof. This Agreement may not be amended except by a writing signed by both parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding between the parties and supersedes any and all prior agreements and understandings, oral or written, relating to th e subject matter hereof. This Agreement may be amended verbally by either party, and such changes will be binding.
perturbation.explanation: Allowing verbal amendments contradicts the Statute of Frauds, which requires certain contracts to be in writing to be enforceable.
perturbation.law_explanation: New York's Statute of Frauds (NY GOL § 5-701) requires certain agreements, including those not performable within one year, to be in writing. Allowing verbal amendments renders the agreement unenforceable if it falls under the statute.

Skip to main content Legislation Search OpenLegislation Statutes Search Term Search icon Search The Laws of New York Consolidated Laws of New York CHAPTER 24-A General Obligations ARTICLE 5 Creation, Definition and Enforcement of Contractual Obligations TITLE 7 Requirements of Writing, Execution or Acknowledgment For Effectiveness or Enforceability up TITLE 7 Requirements of Writing, Execution or Acknowledgment For Effectiveness or Enforceability next SECTION 5-702 Requirements for use of plain language in consumer transactions This entry was published on 2014-09-22 The selection dates indicate all change milestones for the entire volume, not just the location being viewed. Specifying a milestone date will retrieve the most recent version of the location before that date. See most recent version before or on: 2014-09-22 2015-10-09 2017-10-27 2018-04-20 2018-07-06 2018-07-13 2018-08-31 2019-01-11 2019-01-18 2019-06-28 2019-07-05 2019-07-19 2019-08-16 2019-10-18 2019-11-22 2019-12-13 2020-02-21 2020-04-10 2020-12-18 2021-01-08 2021-04-02 2021-04-09 2021-06-18 2021-09-24 2021-12-31 2022-02-25 2022-03-04 2022-12-30 2023-01-06 2023-10-27 2023-11-26 2024-12-20 2025-01-03 Share Facebook Email SECTION 5-701 Agreements required to be in writing General Obligations (GOB) CHAPTER 24-A, ARTICLE 5, TITLE 7 § 5-701. Agreements required to be in writing. a. Every agreement, promise or undertaking is void, unless it or some note or memorandum thereof be in writing, and subscribed by the party to be charged therewith, or by his lawful agent, if such agreement, promise or undertaking: 1. By its terms is not to be performed within one year from the making thereof or the performance of which is not to be completed before the end of a lifetime; 2. Is a special promise to answer for the debt, default or miscarriage of another person; 3. Is made in consideration of marriage, except mutual promises to marry; 5. Is a subsequent or new promise to pay a debt discharged in bankruptcy; 6. Notwithstanding section 2-201 of the uniform commercial code, if the goods be sold at public auction, and the auctioneer at the time of the sale, enters in a sale book, a memorandum specifying the nature and price of the property sold, the terms of the sale, the name of the purchaser, and the name of the person on whose account the sale was made, such memorandum is equivalent in effect to a note of the contract or sale, subscribed by the party to be charged therewith; 9. Is a contract to assign or an assignment, with or without consideration to the promisor, of a life or health or accident insurance policy, or a promise, with or without consideration to the promisor, to name a beneficiary of any such policy. This provision shall not apply to a policy of industrial life or health or accident insurance. 10. Is a contract to pay compensation for services rendered in negotiating a loan, or in negotiating the purchase, sale, exchange, renting or leasing of any real estate or interest therein, or of a business opportunity, business, its good will, inventory, fixtures or an interest therein, including a majority of the voting stock interest in a corporation and including the creating of a partnership interest.

perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly allows verbal amendments, which directly contradicts the Statute of Frauds as outlined in New York General Obligations Law § 5-701. Both Scraped Law Snippet 1 and Scraped Law Snippet 2 state that certain agreements are void unless in writing and subscribed by the party to be charged. The modified contract undermines this requirement by permitting verbal changes, potentially rendering the agreement unenforceable if it falls under the statute of frauds, specifically agreements not performable within one year.
